title: Original EoE
wikitext:
[]: Original EoE             Quote Post by evils_death » Wed Apr 29, 2009 2:48 am I heard that EoE is supposed to have taken place in the original TV series. Until recently, I have merely thought “thank god” and felt lucky they didn’t have to do the seemingly impossible job of cramming it all into two episodes, which seems impossible. However, I have recently mused; how the heck would Anno do it? My mind flew back to EoE and started thinking which scenes wouldn’t have existed if it was only a TV series. Then it dawned on me. The fight scene between Unit-02 and the MPE was just to fill time. Not only does this justify Anno’s brutal killing of Asuka, but would make the idea that unit-01’s late activation was merely something necessary for the storyline make more sense (of course, the idea of evil Yui still makes sense). Plus the idea of Shinji busting out to save Asuka, who is useless in the damaged unit-02, sounds more satisfying than Asuka being completely destroyed by the lances. For some mysterious reason, Kaworu seemed to have let unit-02 get destroyed on purpose. In EoE this seemed to kill the theory unit-02 was important as it was simply repaired in an extremely short period of time. Whether or not this is true and what importance unit-02 has is probably something we cannot guess and will never know (unless if it is explored in rebuild…). Heck it can be possible that unit-02 had no actual use in instrumentality and SEELE just told Kaworu some BS, but it makes sense of some of Kaworu’s lines. Another interesting point: would Ritsuko’s scenes have been cut out? Somehow I doubt it, although to make up for the time they have in the movie they would have needed to add a lot of scenes. Now, lets see some more knowledgeable people completely rip this apart.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Apr 29, 2009 4:31 am Eva-02 had no part in instrumentality. It's goal was to defeat the Angels. Nobody's really sure what Kaworu was told by Seele to do. You can go through the mega episode 24/kaworu/seele/lilith/adam hoopla thread , but one thing to note is that in 24', Seele indicates that they've been betrayed after Kaworu hijacks Eva-02. which would seem to indicate that whole ordeal wasn't part of what Seele wanted him to do. As far as what was originally planned for the ending that made its way into EoE, there's a set of animatics (rushed animation, usually just sketched animation with hardly any color) of an "Episode 25" which includes : Reichu wrote: Okay, the 25' segment in the bonus disc rush video is about 17 minutes long, starts with the JSSDF getting buzzed in the forest, ends right after Ritsuko's shot. Platinum only has 9:38 minutes of the animatic, starting with the harpies' landing, hence why Shin-seiki didn't see Misato shooting the soldier's brains out -- that happens before.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 29, 2009 6:04 am Ornette: The animatics were for used for EoE (episode 25' ). People often cite it as *direct* evidence that that an episode 25'-type ending was being made for television, because the aspect ratio is wrong for the cinema, but that's just because the animatics aren't drawn/filmed "in letterbox". If you look hard enough, though, in most of the shots you can see that the layout sketches are drawn on paper that has lines showing where the letterboxing will be. This includes the oft-reposted image of Misato with the dead JSSDF solder. So the animatics don't really prove anything on their own, unfortunately.

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Wed Apr 29, 2009 6:53 am probably a lot of it would have been cut out, whether it is useful or not. Anno probably hadn't cut it down properly before they had to scrap it, so much of the movie wouldn't be there at all. Everyone would think it was very rushed, and a horrible end to the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Marzan » Wed Apr 29, 2009 8:44 am I think a lot of what we saw in E of E was scripted after the end of the series. I don't think a lot of it would have come in at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Wed Apr 29, 2009 10:05 am Except the idea of Rei returning to nothing has been mentioned before, the MP Evas had been mentioned in Episode 22, Seele's malevolent intentions for Nerv's personnel were revealed in Episode 24, Adam was on Gendo's hand, etc. Clearly all the background for it happened. As for how the Episodes would have played out, except they probably would have been abridged. Less obvious carnage on the human scale, the MP Evas win quicker, and Rei puts up with less before merging. 25 ends slightly differently: Rei's emergence, not the overall situation, is what causes Shinji to end the episode screaming. 26, Shinji/Rei destroys humanity, the weird moments of Instrumentality (the drawings, the sandbox) are probably not present, and Shinji calls the whole thing off quicker. Maybe.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Wed Apr 29, 2009 12:40 pm Reichu wrote: So the animatics don't really prove anything on their own, unfortunately.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Apr 29, 2009 1:28 pm The original script for episodes 25 and 26 show a lot of differences from what we got on air. For instance, Shinji looks to be on the verge of death, when Asuka returns to give him strength, which he responds by screaming, "A 0% chance of victory is more like 100% to the Dai Eva Dan." And then Eva-01 combines with the other Evas, they use an ultimate berserk Giga Drill Breaker while screaming, "Who the hell do you think we are!" and then rip through the space time continuum to land in another dimension. Unsure what to do next, I whistled for a cab and when it came near the license plate said "fresh" and it had dice in the mirror. If anything I could say that this cab is rare, but I thought, "Naw, forget it. Yo homes, to Bel Air." I pulled up to the house about 7 or 8, and I yelled to the cabbie, "Yo homes smell ya later." I looked at my kingdom, I was finally there, to sit on my throne as the Prince of Bel Air.

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Wed Apr 29, 2009 1:53 pm Alaska Slim wrote: Reichu wrote: So the animatics don't really prove anything on their own, unfortunately.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Apr 29, 2009 2:15 pm BrikHaus wrote: The original script for episodes 25 and 26 show a lot of differences from what we got on air. For instance, Shinji looks to be on the verge of death, when Asuka returns to give him strength, which he responds by screaming, "A 0% chance of victory is more like 100% to the Dai Eva Dan." And then Eva-01 combines with the other Evas, they use an ultimate berserk Giga Drill Breaker while screaming, "Who the hell do you think we are!" and then rip through the space time continuum to land in another dimension. Unsure what to do next, I whistled for a cab and when it came near the license plate said "fresh" and it had dice in the mirror. If anything I could say that this cab is rare, but I thought, "Naw, forget it. Yo homes, to Bel Air." I pulled up to the house about 7 or 8, and I yelled to the cabbie, "Yo homes smell ya later." I looked at my kingdom, I was finally there, to sit on my throne as the Prince of Bel Air.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Apr 29, 2009 2:59 pm Jimmy wrote: That preview was only in 24' and I'm not even sure if that was the case before Renewal/Platinum

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Apr 29, 2009 3:18 pm AuraTwilight wrote: I was trying to take this seriously

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 29, 2009 6:14 pm Legendary wrote: Adam was on Gendo's hand

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Wed Apr 29, 2009 6:41 pm Oh. I thought we'd had a shot of Adam on Gendo's hand at a different point (maybe Episode 21.... which now that I'm thinking about it, also has a DC episode.) Well, the other points I made are still valid.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 29, 2009 10:21 pm Legendary wrote: Oh. I thought we'd had a shot of Adam on Gendo's hand at a different point (maybe Episode 21.... which now that I'm thinking about it, also has a DC episode.)

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Wed Apr 29, 2009 11:29 pm According to the proposal, Instrumentality was to span four episodes. I wonder if this reflects on what we received in the end... Also, didn't EVA2 include variants of EOE based on unused screenplay? That might reflect some light into what the intentions were.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Wed Apr 29, 2009 11:51 pm Synapsid wrote: According to the proposal, Instrumentality was to span four episodes. I wonder if this reflects on what we received in the end...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 30, 2009 12:28 am Legendary wrote: No, in Proposal, Instrumentality definitely didn't happen in Episode 23 or 24, and the UN annuls the project in Episode 25. Instrumentality doesn't happen at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Thu Apr 30, 2009 12:45 am I have, and on second thought, the last episode could be an instrumentality sort of set-up, but it definitely isn't four episodes (The UN wouldn't be able withdraw support if it had already happened and they were part of it).

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 30, 2009 1:22 pm Legendary wrote: Someone who has actually played the game should correct me if wrong though.
